title: lablab purpureus var. lignosus
intro: Lablab purpureus var. lignosus, also known as Indian bean or tree bean, is a perennial legume native to tropical Asia and Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to various health benefits such as reducing blood sugar levels, lowering blood pressure, and improving cardiovascular health. Lablab purpureus var. lignosus can be prepared in various forms, including herbal tea, powder, and capsules, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's roots, seeds, and leaves are commonly used in herbal preparations, and it is also used as a food source in some parts of the world, providing protein and fiber-rich beans.
